panels.title: Emails sent (24h)
panels.description: Real emails on the wire = delivered + empty ('all caught up' notes). Uses bulletin_digests_total so empty windows are counted.

panels.title: Subscribers due now
panels.description: Build/digest are decoupled — this is exactly what the next tick will dispatch. Persistently >0 means the generate_digest worker is stalled.

panels.title: Build lag
panels.description: Content freshness, NOT a delivery blocker (digests no longer gate on the build). Lower severity than queue/due backlog.

panels.title: Errored connections
panels.description: Connections in the 'errored' state — silent failures otherwise.

panels.title: Job duration p50 / p95 / p99
panels.description: Real histogram quantiles — bulletin_job_duration_seconds now ships explicit buckets (set in metric::init), so histogram_quantile works and aggregates across replicas.

panels.title: Job retries
panels.description: apalis attempt > 1. Sustained retries flag a flapping dependency before the final attempt fails outright.

panels.title: Oldest pending age
panels.description: Age of the oldest runnable Pending task. The truest 'stuck worker' signal: a small queue that never drains shows up here, not in depth.

panels.title: Unbuilt events
panels.description: Public events ingested past the build watermark, not yet clustered.

panels.title: Events ingested by source & intake
panels.description: New events appended per second, split by intake: 'poll' (the reconciliation backstop) vs 'webhook' (the realtime path). A webhook line that goes flat while poll keeps climbing means the realtime path is down and the poll is silently covering for it.

targets.expr: sum by (source) (rate(bulletin_events_deduplicated_total[5m])) / clamp_min(sum by (source) (rate(bulletin_events_ingested_total[5m])) + sum by (source) (rate(bulletin_events_deduplicated_total[5m])), 1)

panels.title: Time since last ingest
panels.description: Independent liveness signal: jobs can succeed while ingesting nothing. Alert if this climbs past your slowest poll interval.

panels.title: Digest outcomes
panels.description: delivered = real digest; empty = 'all caught up' email; already_delivered/not_yet_due send nothing. A rising empty share signals candidate starvation.

panels.title: Time since last delivery
panels.description: Delivery-side freshness. With build decoupled, this is the trustworthy 'are subscribers getting mail' signal.

panels.title: LLM call error ratio (5m)
panels.description: Non-ok outcomes over all sidecar calls (bulletin_llm_call_duration_seconds_count, the histogram's per-outcome count). Summarization is mandatory (§3.7) — a failed call is a tracked error that retries and, if persistent, quarantines the cluster/story and (for the lead) defers the digest, so a low steady rate is tolerable but a spike means the local sidecar is down (connect), slow (timeout), or erroring and digests are being held back.

panels.title: Cache hit ratio (5m)
panels.description: Content-hash cache hits over all cache decisions (bulletin_llm_cache_total, cluster + story sweeps). A hit reuses the cached summary and makes no model call — this is how much the §3.3 idempotency is saving. Higher is better; a low ratio means content is churning and every sweep is paying for fresh generations.

panels.title: Gate rejection ratio (5m)
panels.description: Faithfulness-gate rejections over successful summarize+synthesize calls. A rejection means the model's candidate was dropped to the deterministic baseline (hallucinated entity/number, hype word, or over-length) — the digest never ships it. A rising ratio is a model-quality signal, not an outage.

panels.title: Completion tokens/s (5m)
panels.description: Generated-token throughput across all phases (bulletin_llm_tokens_total{kind="completion"}). The directest read on how hard the sidecar is working; pair with call latency to spot a model that's slow because it's generating more, not because it's starved.

panels.title: LLM call latency p50 / p95 / p99 by phase
panels.description: Real histogram quantiles of per-call wall time, by phase (summarize | comprehend | synthesize | label | delta). bulletin_llm_call_duration_seconds ships explicit buckets (set in metric::init), so histogram_quantile works and aggregates across replicas. Filtered to outcome="ok" so failure latencies (instant connect, 120s timeout) don't distort the prompt-latency distribution — drop the filter to see failures too, or break down by outcome on the next panel.

panels.title: LLM calls by phase & outcome
panels.description: Per-second sidecar calls split by phase and outcome (ok | timeout | connect | status | decode | transport | response), from the latency histogram's _count series. 'connect' = sidecar down, 'timeout' = sidecar slow, 'response' = answered but unusable (malformed/empty).

panels.title: Token throughput by phase & kind (tokens/s)
panels.description: Prompt vs completion tokens per second, by phase (bulletin_llm_tokens_total). A completion line pinned flat against the phase's max_tokens budget is the 'reasoning ran out the budget' signal — the empty-completion failure mode the client guards against.

panels.title: Cache hit ratio by kind
panels.description: Content-hash hit ratio split by sweep kind (cluster | story). A cluster's hash moves on content change; a story's signature moves on member-summary change. Diverging lines tell you which layer is churning.

panels.title: Gate rejections by phase & reason
panels.description: Faithfulness-gate rejections per second, split by phase (summarize | synthesize) and reason (ungrounded_entity | ungrounded_number | banned_word | url_in_prose | too_long). The reason is the violation variant only — never the offending value — so the series stays bounded. A reason spiking points at a specific model failure mode.
